_comment: Curated heritage colours for pre-current-grid F1 champion constructors, so historic rows in the Champions tab (Drivers' + Constructors' sections) get a plausible team colour too. Keys are the slugified team name as it appears in champions.json (see lib/slug.ts). `color` is a plausible team-heritage hex; `page` (optional) is a /teams/<slug> page target — omitted here because none of these teams have a current profile page, so the consumer colours the text and renders a <span>, not a broken <Link>. `note` documents the heritage rationale and is ignored by code. Colours are plausible identities, not canonical PMS/hex — historic F1 liveries have no official codes (paint sprayed in layers; national racing colours predate sponsorship). Current-grid teams keep priority: these only fill slugs drivers.json doesn't already cover.

brabham.note: Australian racing green-and-gold of owner Jack Brabham; 1966-67 constructor titles.

lotus.note: Team Lotus — JPS black-and-gold gold accent is the enduring signature; BRG in the early-60s title era. Gold reads clearly on the dark background.

tyrrell.note: Elf-era blue, brightened for AA contrast on the dark UI; 1971 constructors' + 1971/73 drivers' titles (Stewart).

matra.note: Bleu de France / French racing blue; 1969 constructors' + drivers' title (Matra-Ford, Stewart).

brm.note: British Racing Motors' metallic green, brightened for AA contrast on the dark UI; 1962 constructors' + drivers' title (Graham Hill).

cooper.note: Cooper's lighter racing green (with red flash); 1959-60 constructor titles (Brabham).

vanwall.note: British Racing Green; won the inaugural 1958 Constructors' Championship.

brawn-gp.note: Fluorescent yellow of the white/yellow/black BGP 001; 2009 double title.

renault.note: Renault's signature yellow (blue-and-yellow in the 2005-06 title years, Alonso).

benetton.note: Benetton's own green, retained on the sidepods through the 1994-95 title era; the team's heritage identity (blue was sponsor Mild Seven).

alfa-romeo.note: Rosso corsa, deep; 1950-51 drivers' titles (Farina, Fangio). Darker than the current Ferrari red so it stays distinct.

maserati.note: Maserati's oxblood-leaning rosso corsa; 1957 drivers' title (Fangio).
